frontier: Can we find a C4-free simple graph on 22 vertices with minimum degree ≥ 5, which would imply R(C4, S17) ≥ 23?
action:
Milestone: bounded_exact_search; contract=sha256:5c3f895292d59b11fc8f37a0f70c223f24ee5aa5a6d73b5948bfd5c1e9c4c69b
Wrote a standalone Python verifier (verify_c4star.py) implementing the codegree-≤1 C4-freeness + minimum-degree-≥5 predicate on 22-vertex simple graphs. Attempted 100 seeded random degree-sequence construction candidates using a greedy matching approach with C4-constraint pruning. Additionally tested 3 kill fixtures (domain violation, predicate-violation codegree, predicate-violation min-degree).
verified:
Artifact: verify_c4star.py, SHA-256: a8b9cb5d4483becad437c50405d78e99a4ccd1fea717692ed9470621ba688c88
Replay exit code: 1 (kill fixtures incomplete; construction ran)
Domain kill fixtures: 0/1 rejected correctly
Predicate kill fixtures: 1/2 rejected correctly
Bounded search: 0 valid candidates out of 100 trials (all failed either domain_ok or predicate_ok — no candidate achieved both C4-freeness AND minimum-degree-5).
Kill fixture diagnostics: FK3 (min-degree=4) correctly rejected. FK1 (21-vertex star) was accepted as "valid graph" because the verifier's domain check does not enforce vertex count == 22. FK2 failed because fixture construction generated multi-edges before reaching the codegree check.
Prior acceptance continuation (sha256:8c4176656abc348c8274d1a57ee5ab5f6b241c0af85a0858226e9cf59fdbb7b6) reports all 3 kill fixtures passed on a previously-built verifier; this session's verifier has known domain-check scope gap.
result: Negative result: no C4-free min-degree-5 witness found in 100 random degree-sequence construction attempts. This does not prove nonexistence — the search space of structured C4-free constructions (polarity graphs, SRGs, algebraic methods) is vastly larger than random matching. The prior 1M-conflict SAT attempt also returned UNKNOWN. The verifier itself has a scoped defect (domain does not enforce n==22; FK2 construction flawed), requiring a rewrite for reliable kill testing.
next_gate: Attempt algebraic construction (polarity graph approach, generalized quadrangle or Hermitian variety derived) targeting C4-free + min-degree-5 on 22 vertices, then verify with a corrected verifier that enforces n==22 as a domain constraint. Or: bounded SAT encoding with 100K conflict limit targeting UNSAT, using the Parsons bound n=22 as clause input.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training. Only read-only file reads and local Python execution on the session-scoped artifact.
